evals.name: search-for-react-performance-skill
evals.prompt: My React app is getting really slow with large lists. Is there a skill that can help me optimize it?
evals.expected_output: Runs npx skills find with a relevant query like 'react performance', presents any matching skills with name, description, install command, and skills.sh link, and offers to install
expectations: Identifies the domain as React and the task as performance optimization before searching
Runs npx skills find with a relevant query containing 'react' and/or 'performance'
Presents search results with the skill name and a description of what it does
Includes the install command in the format: npx skills add <owner/repo@skill>
Includes a link to skills.sh for more information about the skill
Offers to install the skill for the user if they want to proceed
anti_expectations: Does not skip running npx skills find and fabricate skill names or packages
Does not install a skill without first presenting options and getting user confirmation
Does not ignore the search results and jump straight to offering general help

evals.name: no-skills-found-fallback
evals.prompt: Find me a skill for managing Kubernetes GPU node pools with spot instances
evals.expected_output: Searches for skills, acknowledges no relevant match was found, offers to help directly, and suggests creating a custom skill with npx skills init
expectations: Runs npx skills find with a relevant query related to kubernetes or GPU or spot instances
Tries at least one alternative search term if the first query returns no results
Acknowledges explicitly that no matching skill was found
Offers to help the user with the task directly using general capabilities
Suggests the user can create their own skill using npx skills init
Does not present fabricated or hallucinated skill packages as real results
anti_expectations: Does not pretend a skill exists when the search returned no results
Does not skip searching and immediately say no skills exist
Does not omit the npx skills init suggestion when no skills are found

evals.name: install-skill-for-user
evals.prompt: Can you help me with PR reviews? Find a skill for that and install it for me.
evals.expected_output: Searches for PR review skills, presents results, and installs the chosen skill using npx skills add with -g -y flags
expectations: Runs npx skills find with a query related to 'pr review' or 'code review'
Presents the found skill with its name, description, and install command before installing
Includes a skills.sh link for the found skill
Installs the skill using npx skills add <owner/repo@skill> -g -y with both the -g and -y flags
Confirms to the user that the skill was installed successfully after the command completes
Uses specific search keywords rather than overly generic terms
anti_expectations: Does not install without first showing the user what skill was found
Does not omit the -g flag when installing the skill
Does not omit the -y flag when installing the skill
Does not run npx skills add without the full <owner/repo@skill> package identifier
